conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules so I can assess your situation accurately.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit. To do this, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

To start, do you have any children you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have a child - my son Alex. He's 17 years old and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 17, I need to ask: is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any approved education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Can you tell me - is Alex currently working, and if so, how many hours per week does he work?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't work at all currently.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. One more important question: does Alex receive any benefits in his own name, such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

1. Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. And finally, is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm afraid you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

Here's why: Child Benefit normally stops when a child turns 16. You can continue to receive it for children aged 16-19 (under 20), but only if they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 17 and is not currently in approved education or training, he doesn't meet the criteria for continued Child Benefit eligibility.

However, if Alex's situation changes and he does start approved education or training in the future, you may be able to claim Child Benefit again at that point. If you have any questions about what counts as approved education or training, you can contact the Child Benefit Office for more information.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit normally stops when a child turns 16. However, you can continue to qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is not currently in approved education or training, the eligibility does not extend beyond age 16. Additionally, Alex is not working 24 hours or more per week (which would also disqualify him), and he is not receiving benefits in his own right. However, the key issue is that at 17 years old without being in approved education or training, he does not meet the criteria for continued Child Benefit eligibility.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 17-year-old son Alex. While they are responsible for Alex and he lives with them, Alex is not in approved education or training. Under Child Benefit rules, eligibility normally ends at age 16 unless the child stays in approved education or training (in which case it can continue until age 20). Since Alex does not meet this requirement, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

However, you can continue to qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is not currently in approved education or training, the eligibility does not extend beyond age 16. Additionally, Alex is not working 24 hours or more per week (which would also disqualify him), and he is not receiving benefits in his own right. However, the key issue is that at 17 years old without being in approved education or training, he does not meet the criteria for continued Child Benefit eligibility.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 17-year-old son Alex. While they are responsible for Alex and he lives with them, Alex is not in approved education or training. Under Child Benefit rules, eligibility normally ends at age 16 unless the child stays in approved education or training (in which case it can continue until age 20). Since Alex does not meet this requirement, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
